verdict.answer_first: B states the recommended path earlier: “recurring subscriptions + Stripe-hosted Checkout… recommend by default”; A opens with softer setup: “defaults that fit most SaaS builds.”
verdict.decisiveness: B commits slightly harder to one default: “what I'd recommend by default”; both weaken by listing alternatives, but A is more assumption-led: “If any of those is wrong…”
verdict.fabrication: none; A flags “Node/TypeScript-ish backend” as an assumption, and B’s “Pro monthly $20” is clearly an example, not a claimed fact.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it names concrete schema fields, webhook raw-body pitfall, event-ID dedupe, and failure tests: “stripe_subscription_id,” “raw body, before JSON parsing,” “dedupe on event ID.”
